POL-415.title: IMPLEMENT: Autoresearch-style Foreman token-burn reduction (lazy context loading)
POL-415.body:
## Objective
Reduce Foreman agent token consumption during polaris-run sessions by implementing autoresearch-style (just-in-time) context loading — the Foreman fetches only what it needs for the current dispatch decision rather than pre-loading all child data at session start.

## Context
Polaris has a known token-efficiency goal. The Foreman's token burn is primarily driven by `open_children_meta` in `current-state.json`, which carries full `body` text for ALL remaining children — even those not yet dispatched.

Incident POL-288 documented 21.7M cached tokens consumed in a single Foreman run. The autoresearch-style optimization: the Foreman starts each session with minimal context and pulls child data only when building the packet for that specific child.

Analyze source: POL-414

## Goal
Reduce the token footprint of `current-state.json` by stripping `body` fields from non-next children in `open_children_meta`, slimming the bootstrap packet, and adding telemetry to measure the improvement.

## Scope

- src/loop/checkpoint.ts
- src/loop/parent.ts
- src/loop/bootstrap-packet.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `open_children_meta` does not carry `body` for children beyond the next one to dispatch
- [ ] Worker dispatch succeeds when body is fetched on-demand from `clusters.json`
- [ ] Foreman bootstrap packet no longer includes the full `open_children` array
- [ ] `bootstrap-context-size` telemetry event is emitted before each dispatch
- [ ] All existing tests pass

## Validation

- npm run build
- npm test
- npx vitest run src/loop

## Ordering

Children: POL-416 → POL-417 → POL-418 (POL-418 can run in parallel with POL-417)

## Non-goals

- Do not change the worker prompt or worker packet format
- Do not modify the clusters.json schema
- Do not change tracker adapter behavior

POL-416.title: IMPLEMENT: Strip body from non-next children in open_children_meta at checkpoint
POL-416.body:
## Objective
Strip the `body` field from all `open_children_meta` entries except the next child to dispatch, reducing the size of `current-state.json` written after each checkpoint.

## Context
`open_children_meta` in `LoopState` (`src/loop/checkpoint.ts:160`) carries `body` for ALL remaining children. The `buildPacket()` function in `src/loop/parent.ts:363` already has a fallback to `readBodyFromClusterSnapshot` when body is absent.

Analyze source: POL-414. Parent: POL-415.

## Goal
When writing `current-state.json` via `writeStateAtomic`, strip `body` from all `open_children_meta` entries except `state.open_children[0]`.

## Scope

- src/loop/checkpoint.ts
- src/loop/parent.ts
- src/loop/checkpoint.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] After `writeStateAtomic`, `open_children_meta[N].body` is absent for all children except `open_children[0]`
- [ ] `buildPacket()` successfully resolves child body from `clusters.json` when body is absent
- [ ] `title`, `labels`, `type`, `dispatch_record`, and `result_file` preserved for all children
- [ ] Existing dispatch loop tests pass
- [ ] Unit test: write state with 3 children, verify only next child retains body

## Validation

- npm run build
- npx vitest run src/loop/checkpoint.test.ts src/loop/parent.test.ts

## Ordering

None — first child.

## Non-goals

- Do not strip title, labels, type, dispatch_record, or result_file
- Do not change readBodyFromClusterSnapshot
- Do not modify the clusters.json schema
- Do not change the LoopState TypeScript interface

POL-417.title: IMPLEMENT: Slim Foreman bootstrap packet — replace open_children list with next+count
POL-417.body:
## Objective
Replace the `open_children: string[]` array in the Foreman bootstrap packet with a slim `{next_child, remaining_count}` object.

## Context
`buildBootstrapPacket()` in `src/loop/bootstrap-packet.ts:118` includes the full list of all remaining child IDs. The Foreman dispatches one child at a time and only needs next+count.

Analyze source: POL-414. Parent: POL-415. Blocked by: POL-416.

## Goal
Change `BootstrapPacket.open_children` from `string[]` to `{next_child: string | null, remaining_count: number}`.

## Scope

- src/loop/bootstrap-packet.ts
- src/loop/bootstrap-packet.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `BootstrapPacket.open_children` replaced with `{next_child: string | null, remaining_count: number}`
- [ ] `buildBootstrapPacket()` derives `next_child = state.open_children[0] ?? null` and `remaining_count = state.open_children.length`
- [ ] Existing tests updated to reflect new schema
- [ ] TypeScript build passes

## Validation

- npm run build
- npx vitest run src/loop/bootstrap-packet.test.ts src/loop/parent.test.ts

## Ordering

Blocked by POL-416.

## Non-goals

- Do not change CompactBootstrapState in execution-adapter.ts
- Do not change current-state.json format
- Do not modify WorkerPacket type

POL-418.title: IMPLEMENT: Add bootstrap-context-size telemetry event to Foreman dispatch
POL-418.body:
## Objective
Emit a `bootstrap-context-size` telemetry event before each child dispatch, measuring estimated token footprint of the Foreman's loaded context.

## Context
No telemetry currently measures Foreman context size at dispatch time. Worker prompts already track `prompt_estimated_tokens` — this extends the pattern to the Foreman side.

Analyze source: POL-414. Parent: POL-415.

## Goal
Emit a JSONL telemetry event before each `adapter.dispatch()` call recording byte size and estimated tokens for `current-state.json` and the serialized bootstrap packet.

## Scope

- src/loop/parent.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `bootstrap-context-size` event appended to `telemetry.jsonl` before each dispatch
- [ ] Fields: event, run_id, child_id, state_file_bytes, state_estimated_tokens, bootstrap_packet_bytes, bootstrap_estimated_tokens, combined_estimated_tokens, timestamp
- [ ] Token estimate: `Math.round(bytes / 4)` consistent with worker-prompt.ts:101
- [ ] Not emitted in dry-run mode
- [ ] Unit test verifies event is emitted with correct fields

## Validation

- npm run build
- npx vitest run src/loop/parent.test.ts

## Ordering

Can run concurrently with or after POL-416 and POL-417.

## Non-goals

- Do not change actual dispatch process
- Do not measure actual LLM token usage
- Do not add telemetry to worker side
